serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Kerupuk Garlic Crackers is about 10 pieces, weighing approximately 50 grams.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values adapted from 'Keropok' (fish crackers) and 'Tapioca crackers' entries in HPB Foodo, as a direct entry for 'Garlic Crackers' was not available. Values adjusted to reflect a fried, garlic-flavored cracker profile.

gi_source.title: University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: Estimated based on 'Tapioca crackers' (GI 75) from the University of Sydney GI Database, as Kerupuk Garlic Crackers are primarily made from tapioca flour and are deep-fried, which generally maintains a high GI.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium and saturated fat content
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid Kerupuk Garlic Crackers due to their very high sodium content (700mg/100g) and significant saturated fat (16g/100g), both of which contribute to elevated blood pressure and cardiovascular risk.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index and refined carbohydrates
type2_diabetes.guidance: Kerupuk Garlic Crackers are unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to their high glycemic index (75), high carbohydrate content (46g/100g) from refined tapioca flour, and low dietary fiber (1g/100g), which can lead to rapid blood glucose spikes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, and sodium content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: This food poses a high risk for individuals with cardiovascular disease due to its high saturated fat (16g/100g), trans fat (0.2g/100g), and sodium (700mg/100g), all of which are detrimental to heart health and can worsen existing conditions.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation (AGEs), high saturated fat, and sodium
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid these crackers as deep-frying generates advanced glycation end products (AGEs) that exacerbate inflammation, and the high saturated fat and sodium content can further promote inflammatory responses and tissue swelling.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kerupuk Garlic Crackers are a popular Indonesian snack, known for their crispy texture and savory garlic flavor. These crackers are typically made from tapioca flour, garlic, and salt, then deep-fried to a golden perfection. They are commonly served as an accompaniment to various Indonesian meals, adding a satisfying crunch. Nutritionally, they are high in calories, fat (especially saturated fat), carbohydrates, and sodium, with a high glycemic index due to the refined tapioca flour. This makes them generally unsuitable for individuals managing conditions like hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from similar items in HPB Foodo, and GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database, as exact entries for 'Kerupuk Garlic Crackers' were not found in primary approved sources. The description and cultural context are well-established.
